93.reason: [The variable 'c' is used before being initialized. The first check 'if(c==EOF)' uses an uninitialized value, which leads to undefined behavior.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The variable 'c' is used before being initialized. The first check 'if(c==EOF)' uses an uninitialized value, which leads to undefined behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [The variable 'c' is used uninitialized in the first iteration of the loop; the check 'if(c==EOF)' happens before reading the first character, leading to undefined behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 1.0 , reason:  [The variable 'c' is used before being initialized in the first iteration of the loop, leading to undefined behavior. The check 'if(c==EOF)' should occur after reading.]

125.reason: [After reading the last word with fscanf, fgetc reads the next character which may be EOF, but if the file ends exactly after the last word without whitespace, this may skip the last word or cause premature exit. Also, mixing fscanf and fgetc without proper synchronization can lead to missed or duplicated reads.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [After reading the last word with fscanf, fgetc reads the next character which may be EOF, but if the file ends exactly after the last word without whitespace, this may skip the last word or cause premature exit. Also, mixing fscanf and fgetc without proper synchronization can lead to missed or duplicated reads.]
